title: phoradendron englerianum
intro: Phoradendron englerianum, also known as the Mexican mistletoe or San Gabriel mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of trees, such as oak and pine, in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phoradendron englerianum include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as used in tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
